summary: Session 2 (Schema + forced writer): shipped the Set 057 implementation against the locked S1 contract. (1) Added the per-session `type` field (work|verification|remediation, default work; absent==work) to progress.py, preserved across writer rebuilds in session_state.py (_build_sessions_array + _existing_session_types), and documented in session-state-schema.md. (2) Extended sN-issues.json to schemaVersion 2: promoted four optional finding fields (issueId/issueType/verificationMethod/suggestedTestOrCheck) and enum-enforced resolution_status + issueType when present, gated so v1 files stay valid; updated schema JSON, doc, and example fixture. (3) Implemented the blessed writer register_typed_session_start (appends a typed sessions[] entry, grows the runtime totalSessions, fail-loud refusals) plus the `start_session --type` CLI branch with a type-announcement banner that points typed sessions at the workflow doc (closes an operator-raised discoverability gap: typed sessions are not in spec.md). (4) Added ai_router/dedicated_verification.py: the content-aware close-time validator (different-engine verification before terminal close; D3 left unchanged) wired into close_session as a non-blocking advisory (gate STRENGTH deferred to S3), the verificationMode record reader/writer, the sN-issues seeder, and the seven-state derivation helper per the Q3 ladder. (5) Tests for all of it; full Python suite green. Cross-provider session verification (gpt-5-4): round 1 ISSUES_FOUND with three Major correctness findings (validator empty-baseline false positive; advisory-disagreement mis-classified as terminal; seeder accepted a VERIFIED verdict) -- all three real, fixed in-flight with regression tests; round 2 VERIFIED. No packaged ai_router surface released (held for S3 version bump per spec). Carried to S3: the verification->remediation hand-off close transition (a non-terminal verification close yields an all-complete sessions[] that invariant rule 6 rejects), the gate strength (Q6 hard-TTY/soft-non-TTY), the workflow-doc generic procedure for typed sessions, and the copy-prompt wiring.

reason.specifics: S3 stays on opus-4-8 (the active engine). It is a consistency-critical rewrite of docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md + the authoring guide (per-set verification made consistent, bounded rounds, the second-opinion tie-breaker, AND the generic procedure typed sessions follow in lieu of a spec.md step list), plus wiring the operator-choice capture and the Q6 close-out gate strength, plus the held PyPI version bump. The S2 verifier flagged that the derivation's disposition partition and the validator's fail-closed edges are subtle -- keep adversarial cross-provider verification on the S3 changes.
